name: Brother Piner
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears a monk robe and pigskin shoes. Armed with an Akaviri Katana. Carries a copy of The Warp in the West.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: devoted, dutiful, struggling, disciplined, conflicted
Role: A monk living at Weynon Priory with Prior Maborel, Jauffre, and herder Eronor. He serves in the Order of Talos, though he was training to become a Blade before choosing the monastic life. He possesses knowledge of combat arts, as evidenced by his mastery of blocking techniques and his possession of an Akaviri Katana.
Home Location Information: Weynon Priory, where he sleeps and performs his daily duties.
Daily Routine: Brother Piner sleeps between midnight and 1am, wakes briefly, then returns to bed until 5am. He goes down to the chapel and stays there until 10am, when he travels to Chorrol to visit Seed-Neeus at Northern Goods and Trade. Upon entering the store, he immediately leaves and returns to Weynon Priory, where he wanders aimlessly upstairs until 6pm. He then goes downstairs and sits until 8pm, after which he wanders upstairs again until bedtime at midnight.
The Difficult Letter: Several crumpled notes can be found on the table in the priory, revealing Brother Piner's struggle to compose an appropriate letter to his mother. The numerous discarded drafts suggest someone wrestling with what to say, perhaps torn between his current life and family obligations, or simply finding it difficult to express himself in writing.
Personality - The Former Warrior: Brother Piner represents an unusual path—a man who was training to become one of the elite Blades but chose instead the quiet contemplation of monastic life. Yet he still carries an Akaviri Katana and possesses skill in defensive combat, suggesting his warrior training remains part of who he is. His daily trip to Chorrol that ends as soon as it begins hints at unfinished business or unresolved feelings—perhaps an errand he cannot bring himself to complete, or a person he cannot face. The crumpled letters to his mother reveal a man struggling to communicate, possibly about his decision to abandon the Blades for the monastery. His interrupted sleep pattern and aimless wandering suggest an unsettled mind, someone still finding his place in the life he has chosen.
author: Bethesda
